pr.title: Match grInishie1_801FA9B4 to 100% by copying the same structure for StageData found in grHeal
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: Matched grInishie1_801FA9B4 by reshaping nearby stage data in src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c to mirror the StageData layout pattern used in grHeal. The change introduced explicit colocated data for an S16Vec3-style index array, the stage DAT path string, a wrapper struct containing StageData plus the adjacent OSReport format string, and a filename string, then rewrote grInishie1_801FA9B4 to reference callbacks and report strings through that reconstructed data layout.
what_changed: Added grI1_803E48C8, a 22-element s16 array whose values are cast into the StageData S16Vec3 pointer field.
Added grI1_803E4944 as the stage resource path string "/GrI1.dat".
Introduced typedef struct grInishie1_StageData containing a StageData field followed by char report_fmt_get_gobj[0x24].

Added grI1_803E4950, a grInishie1_StageData instance initialized with INISHIE1, the DAT path, a count of 1, the casted S16Vec3 pointer, a count of 7, and the report format string "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n".
Added grI1_803E49A8 as the filename string "grinishie1.c".
Changed grInishie1_801FA9B4 so its StageCallbacks pointer is derived via pointer arithmetic from grI1_803E48C8 plus 0x2c, rather than directly taking &grI1_803E48F4[arg0].
Changed the failed Ground_GetStageGObj path to call OSReport using grI1_803E4950.report_fmt_get_gobj and grI1_803E49A8 instead of inline string literals.
smart_moves: Used an existing matched stage file pattern, identified in the PR title as grHeal, as a template for recovering the StageData-adjacent layout in grInishie1.
Modeled adjacent rodata/data as a wrapper struct rather than treating the report format string as an unrelated standalone symbol, which helped reproduce compiler addressing behavior.
Preserved the logical StageCallbacks table while forcing the generated code to compute the callback pointer from the earlier colocated data address, matching the observed binary layout.
Moved inline OSReport literals into named data symbols so the function referenced the same global storage layout as the original binary.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching a stubborn function may require reconstructing the exact global data packing around StageData, not just correcting the function body.
When a callback table appears adjacent to stage metadata, pointer arithmetic from an earlier symbol can be necessary to match compiler output even if a direct array access is clearer C.
StageData layouts may include neighboring strings such as assert/report format strings; wrapping StageData with trailing character arrays can model that adjacency cleanly.
Cross-stage comparison is valuable: a known-good structure in one ground module, here grHeal according to the PR title, can reveal the intended layout for another stage module.
naming_conventions: Global data symbols in this file use the grI1_803Exxxx address-style naming pattern, such as grI1_803E48C8, grI1_803E4944, grI1_803E4950, and grI1_803E49A8.
The new wrapper typedef is named after the stage module and purpose: grInishie1_StageData.
The wrapper field names describe semantic contents: stage_data and report_fmt_get_gobj.
The stage path string is stored separately as grI1_803E4944 and referenced from the StageData initializer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced direct callback-table indexing, &grI1_803E48F4[arg0], with a casted address calculation: (StageCallbacks*) ((char*) grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c) + arg0.
Used a wrapper struct with a trailing char array of fixed size 0x24 to force the report format string to sit immediately after StageData.
Casted grI1_803E48C8 to S16Vec3* in the StageData initializer to match the expected StageData field type while keeping the underlying data declared as s16.
Hoisted OSReport format and filename strings into globals to match data references rather than emitting or reusing inline literals in the function.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no human text excerpt was included, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other stage modules where StageData is wrapped with trailing report format strings.
Compare grInishie1_StageData layout against the grHeal StageData structure referenced by the PR title.
Search for direct StageCallbacks indexing that might need pointer arithmetic from nearby stage metadata to match.
Audit grInishie1.c for remaining address-style data symbols that could be given semantic struct layouts.
